speeches.name: Michael S. Barr
speeches.summary: Barr discussed central banking and balance sheet issues, emphasizing careful policy implementation and financial stability considerations.

speeches.name: Michelle W. Bowman
speeches.summary: Bowman focused on bank supervision and the migration of corporate lending, suggesting a pragmatic regulatory stance without a clear policy tilt.

speeches.name: Christopher J. Waller
speeches.summary: Waller spoke on Federal Reserve Bank operations, which is typically more operational than macro-policy signaling and appears neutral for rate outlook.

speeches.name: Lisa D. Cook
speeches.summary: Cook addressed tokenization and financial-system implications, a topic that does not materially shift near-term monetary policy expectations.

speeches.name: Jerome H. Powell
speeches.summary: Powell said policy is in a good place, the Fed will wait for incoming data, and the committee will remain attentive to both inflation and labor-market risks.

macro_fred.narrative: Leaning Neutral: CPI at 4.0%, Fed Funds at 3.63%, unemployment at 4.3%.
